name: Formula One 1978
attribution: Historical data derived from f1db (github.com/f1db/f1db, CC BY 4.0)
AI ratings from 'Custom AI by jusk - F1 1978 Season' (F-Retro_Gen2.xml), matching the F1_1978_Season skinpack
notes: Coverage: f1db 1978 entrant Arturo Merzario (merzario+ford, rounds 1,3-4,8,10,13-15) has no livery in the referenced skinpack/AI set and is not included in this pack.

Keke Rosberg drove for ATS (rounds 8-10,15-16), Wolf (rounds 11-13) and Theodore (round 3) across 1978; the source AI set ships one livery, 'ATS-Ford Cosworth #10 K. Rosberg', so his entry and per-round grid membership cover only the ATS stint. His Wolf and Theodore stints are the same driver in an uncovered car.

Rene Arnoux drove for Martini (rounds 6,9,12-13) and Surtees (rounds 15-16) in 1978; the source AI set ships one livery, 'Martini-Ford Cosworth #31 R. Arnoux', so his entry and per-round grid membership cover only the Martini stint.

Riccardo Patrese raced under number 36 at round 2 (Brazil) and number 35 from round 3 on; the source AI livery 'Arrows-Ford Cosworth #35 R. Patrese' and this entry use 35 for all his starts (rounds 2-14,16). He did not start round 15 (US East).

Car-model bindings: Lotus (Andretti/Peterson/Rebaque) -> lotus_79 (the 1978 title-winning Lotus 79, run alongside the 78 early-season) and Brabham (Lauda/Watson) -> brabham_bt46b (the BT46/BT46B 'fan car'); every other team binds the class's generic formula_retro_g2 model. The F-Retro_Gen2 class also contains brabham_bt49 (a 1979 car) which is not used by this 1978 season.
The 1978 Argentine GP ran the Buenos Aires No. 15 combination (5.968 km); AMS2's buenos_aires_15 (Circuito 15, 5.948 km) is that same layout, so the round is a real venue, not a placeholder.
